ops.text: PARTIAL RESULTS (baselines only) + EXECUTION FAILURE + re-run. The encoder-push train lane launched GPU training as a background process then RETURNED (orphaning it, same failure mode as E30.5's sanity subprocess); the encoders (Ettin/DeBERTa/ModernBERT) NEVER produced probs, and the leaked-semaphore crash (MPS + DataLoader multiprocessing) killed the one attempt. VERIFY confirmed the BASELINES to <1e-9 and frame integrity clean (frozen gate_eval n=1325 sha c7f923eb, 0 id/session leak vs the 14770-row train frame, 86 vs 818 disjoint sessions): incumbent gate AUC 0.7161, TF-IDF+logreg 0.7182, rep-stats probe 0.7083, SetFit 0.5401 (near-chance — SetFit failed on this task). FINDING already usable: TF-IDF (0.718) ≈ the sklearn incumbent gate (0.716) — the production gate is essentially a TF-IDF-level linear model; none of {repstats, tfidf, setfit} actionably beats it (repstats Δ=-0.008 CI incl 0; tfidf Δ=+0.002 CI incl 0; setfit Δ=-0.176 actionable WORSE). The open question — can a fine-tuned transformer encoder beat the TF-IDF-level gate — needs the encoder arms, which are being RE-RUN as a detached resumable driver (dataloader_num_workers=0 fixes the leaked-semaphore crash; MPS or CPU per smoke). E36 stays open pending that.
